rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside The Docket Order Dismissing The Entry Of Default Of Ka Man Liu Per Ccp 473(C)(1)(C) And ((D)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 7, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF TONY FU'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DOCKET ORDER DISMISSING THE ENTRY OF DEFAULT OF KAMAN LIU PER CCP 473(C)(1)(C) AND (D) is DENIED. Moving party fails to meet the requirements of CCP 1008. Additionally, the proposed response was served and received by the Court within 6 months of entry of default. Puryear v. Stanley (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 291. The Honorable Charles F. Haines is unavailable to hear this motion at this time. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kaman Liu'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Of Plaintiff'S Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 7, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT KAMAN LIU'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR MANDATORY DISMISSAL OF PLAINTIFF'S ACTION is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to bring this action to trial within 5 years. Per August 30, 2017 Bankruptcy Court order the stay in this action is governed by Bankruptcy Code 362(c), which does not stay actions brought by the bankruptcy debtor to Carley Capital Group v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (D.C. Cir.1989) 889 F.2d 1126. Previously filed Notice of Termination of Bankruptcy included incorrect statement "the stay has been vacated by an order of another Court" and a subsequent order invalidating that filing, doesn't change the fact that under the law the stay never applied in the first place and has always been governed by Bankruptcy Code 362(c) as stated on August 30, 2017 decision. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
